title: rosa canina var. pouzinii
intro: Rosa canina var. pouzinii, also known as the dwarf dog rose or Rosa pouzini, is a subspecies of the Rosa canina species, a type of wild rose that is native to Europe and parts of Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Rosa canina var. pouzinii include its ability to support immune function, reduce inflammation, and improve cardiovascular health, as well as its potential to aid in wound healing and protect against cancer. Herbal preparations of Rosa canina var. pouzinii can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, and are often used to create topical creams and salves for skin and wound care.
